steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage contrasts the realist style (accurately portraying working people) with the romantic style. The blank describes what romantic paintings do to "their subjects’ beauty or heroism while hiding all imperfection." The word must fit: amplifying positives while suppressing negatives.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘exaggerate’: To exaggerate means to represent something as greater or more extreme than it is. Paintings that exaggerate beauty or heroism while hiding flaws amplify the positives and suppress the negatives — exactly the romantic style as described. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘correct’: To correct means to fix errors. “Correcting” beauty or heroism makes no logical sense — beauty and heroism are not mistakes to be fixed. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘undermine’: To undermine means to weaken or erode. Paintings that undermine their subjects’ beauty or heroism would be critical or negative — contradicting “hiding all imperfection.” ✗
  • Choice D — ‘mock’: To mock means to ridicule. Mockery exposes flaws rather than hiding them, which contradicts the passage. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
